---
title: "Shiny Modules"
---

nacre components compose naturally with standard Shiny modules. Use `nacreOutput()` and `renderNacre()` to embed a nacre component tree inside a module's UI and server functions, exactly like any other Shiny output. The reactive state lives inside the module's server function, so each module instance is fully independent.

This example instantiates two counter modules side by side. Each has its own slider, reset button, and a `verbatimTextOutput` showing the server-side value — confirming that state is isolated per instance.

<iframe
  src="../apps/module/"
  width="100%"
  height="380px"
  style="border: 1px solid #dee2e6; border-radius: 4px;"
></iframe>

<details><summary>Show source</summary>

```{r echo=FALSE, results='asis'}
knitr::asis_output(paste0("```r\n", paste(readLines("examples/module.R"), collapse = "\n"), "\n```"))
```

</details>
